title: retrophyllum minus
intro: Retrophyllum minus, also known as the lesser canella or lesser cinnamon, is a tropical evergreen tree native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Podocarpaceae. The bark, leaves, and roots of this plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Retrophyllum minus include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the bark and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness the plant's medicinal properties.
